title: ammannia baccifera
intro: Ammannia baccifera, also known as the Indian shot or shot bush, is a plant species in the family Lythraceae. It is a small shrub native to tropical Asia, and its name comes from the German naturalist Johann Jacob Ammann. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Ammannia baccifera include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in traditional medicine formulations.
